Content Packs

Content packs are JSON files that seed a fresh Outpost install with collections, items, menus, globals, folders, and HTML template files. They power the setup wizard's "starter content" step and deploy directly to the site root.

Overview

Content packs live in the content-packs/ directory inside the outpost/ folder. A manifest file (packs.json) lists available packs with metadata. Individual pack files are named {pack-id}.json.

outpost/
  content-packs/
    packs.json                  # Manifest
    blog.json                   # Blog starter pack
    portfolio.json              # Portfolio starter pack
    business.json               # Business site starter pack

Manifest: packs.json

The manifest is a JSON array of pack definitions:

[
  {
    "id": "blog",
    "name": "Blog",
    "description": "Personal blog with sample posts and categories.",
    "icon": "pencil"
  },
  {
    "id": "portfolio",
    "name": "Portfolio",
    "description": "Portfolio with projects, about page, and contact.",
    "icon": "grid"
  },
  {
    "id": "business",
    "name": "Business",
    "description": "Business site with services, team, and contact form.",
    "icon": "briefcase"
  },
  {
    "id": "blank",
    "name": "Start from scratch",
    "description": "Just the basics — no sample content.",
    "icon": "plus"
  }
]
Field Type Description
id string Unique pack identifier, used as the pack filename
name string Display name shown in the wizard
description string Short description shown below the name
icon string Icon key (pencil, grid, briefcase, plus) for the wizard UI
Note — The blank pack has no corresponding JSON file. When selected, the wizard creates minimal starter files without seeding any content.

Pack file format

Each pack file is a JSON object with optional top-level keys:

{
  "settings": {
    "site_name": "My Blog"
  },
  "globals": [
    { "field_name": "site_tagline", "type": "text", "content": "Welcome to my blog" }
  ],
  "collections": [
    {
      "name": "Posts",
      "slug": "post",
      "schema": [ ... ],
      "url_pattern": "/post/{slug}",
      "items": [
        {
          "title": "Hello World",
          "slug": "hello-world",
          "status": "published",
          "data": { "excerpt": "My first post", "body": "<p>Hello!</p>" }
        }
      ],
      "folders": [
        {
          "name": "Categories",
          "slug": "categories",
          "labels": ["News", "Tutorials", "Personal"]
        }
      ]
    }
  ],
  "menus": [
    {
      "slug": "main",
      "name": "Main Menu",
      "items": [
        { "label": "Home", "url": "/" },
        { "label": "Blog", "url": "/blog" }
      ]
    }
  ]
}

Settings

Key-value pairs inserted into the settings table via INSERT OR REPLACE.

Globals

Array of global field definitions. The pack ensures the __global__ page exists before inserting fields.

FieldTypeDescription
field_namestringThe global field name (e.g. site_tagline)
typestringField type: text, richtext, image, textarea, etc.
contentstringInitial value

Collections

Array of collection definitions, each with optional nested items and folders.

Menus

Array of menu definitions with nested items. Each item has label and url fields.

Where content packs deploy

When a content pack is applied, template files are deployed directly to the site root. CSS, JavaScript, and images go into their respective directories at the site root. Database content (collections, menus, globals) is inserted into the SQLite database.

How packs are applied

The POST setup/apply endpoint processes everything in a single SQLite transaction:

  1. Update site_name in settings
  2. Load the pack JSON file ({pack-id}.json)
  3. Deploy template files to the site root
  4. Insert globals (ensuring __global__ page exists)
  5. Insert collections with schema, items, folders, and labels
  6. Insert menus with items
  7. Set setup_completed = '1'
  8. Clear the template cache

If any step fails, the entire transaction is rolled back and no partial content is left behind.

Creating custom packs

To add a custom content pack:

  1. Create a JSON file following the pack format (e.g. agency.json)
  2. Add an entry to packs.json with the matching id
  3. The pack will appear in the setup wizard
Tip — Content packs are only used during initial setup. They don't affect existing content, and changing a pack file after setup has no effect on the site.
